grammarNotes.body: 始計 (shǐ jì) is a title compound from Sunzi's Art of War, literally 'beginning planning.' 計 (to calculate/plan) functions as a noun here, while 始 (beginning/first) modifies it. This is a verb-object compound used as a chapter title, meaning roughly 'Initial Strategic Assessments' or 'The First Planning.' Compare modern Chinese compounds like 火車 (huǒchē, 'fire-car' = train).

第一 (dì-yī) is an ordinal construction: 第 marks ordinal numbers (first, second, third...) and 一 is the numeral 'one.' Unlike English 'the first,' Chinese places the ordinal marker 第 before the number. This pattern continues in modern Chinese: 第二 (second), 第三 (third), etc.
